title: capability-registry-v1
description: Installed capability registry for the Basidiocarp ecosystem. Producer: Stipe (written after successful install or update). Consumer: Spore (reads to resolve capability ids to binary or endpoint candidates). Each entry names a managed tool, its version, its capability ids, and the transport kind to use when calling it.

written_at_unix.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when Stipe last wrote this registry.

entries.description: Installed capability entries. One entry per managed or detected tool.

manager.description: Who owns this installation. 'stipe' = managed by Stipe install/update/uninstall. 'manual' = user-installed binary outside Stipe. 'self' = tool manages its own installation.

capability_ids.description: Stable capability identifiers this tool satisfies, e.g. ['memory.store.v1', 'memory.recall.v1']. Used by Spore to resolve 'who can do X?'.

contract_ids.description: Related Septa schema contract ids this tool produces or consumes, e.g. ['command-output-v1', 'code-graph-v1']. For cross-reference only; does not imply the tool implements every contract.

transport.description: Preferred transport kind for callers. 'cli' = invoke as a subprocess. 'stdio' = MCP over stdin/stdout. 'unix-socket' or 'tcp' = network endpoint.

binary_path.description: Absolute path to the installed binary when transport is 'cli', 'stdio', or a socket-backed daemon. Null when the tool is not a binary (e.g., a package or plugin).

status.description: Last known health state at registration time. 'ok' = binary was present and passed a probe. 'degraded' = present but probe returned a non-zero result. 'missing' = binary path no longer resolves.

message.description: Human-readable detail about the health state. Set when status is 'degraded' or 'missing'.
